name: DeepSeek-R1
parameter_count: 671B total (37B active per token via MoE)
training_approach: Large-scale pretraining followed by reinforcement learning for reasoning without supervised fine-tuning on chain-of-thought data
key_innovations: Achieved reasoning performance comparable to OpenAI o1 at a fraction of the training cost (under $6M estimated)
Used pure reinforcement learning to develop chain-of-thought reasoning without supervised CoT examples
Demonstrated that MoE architectures with far fewer active parameters can match dense model performance
Open-weight release under MIT license challenged the assumption that frontier AI requires massive budgets
Showed emergent self-verification and reflection behaviors from RL training alone
lineage_path: Transformer -> GPT -> MoE -> DeepSeek-V2 -> DeepSeek-V3 -> DeepSeek-R1
cultural_impact: DeepSeek-R1 sent shockwaves through the AI industry in January 2025, demonstrating that a Chinese lab could produce frontier reasoning capabilities for under $6M in compute — a fraction of what Western labs spent. The release triggered a stock market selloff in AI chip companies and forced a reassessment of the scaling hypothesis. It proved that algorithmic innovation could substitute for brute-force compute scaling.
